title: paramollugo spathulata
intro: Paramollugo spathulata, also known as Indian Mallow or Velvet Leaf, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Molluginaceae, commonly found in the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The leaves and roots of this plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Paramollugo spathulata include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throat, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve digestion. In terms of herbal preparations, Paramollugo spathulata can be consumed as a decoction, made by boiling the leaves and roots in water, or used as a topical application for its antiseptic properties.
